stem: The function g is defined by g(x) = (x + 2)(x − 1)²(x − 3)². If h(x) = g(x − 2), what is the product of all distinct x-intercepts of the graph of y = h(x) in the xy-plane?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Replacing x with x − 2 shifts every x-intercept of g two units to the right. The intercepts of g are at x = −2, 1, 3, so the intercepts of h are at x = 0, 3, 5. Their product is 0 · 3 · 5 = 0.

Choice B is the product 3 · 5 (ignoring the 0 root). Choice C doubles that. Choice D negates a wrong product. Only choice A correctly includes the root at x = 0.
